title: From Scorching Heat to Heavy Rain: Is Your Weekend Travel Plan Safe? - NativePlanet
full_text:
Delhi and the National Capital Region (NCR) face a severe heatwave today. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) warns of rising temperatures across North India. Meanwhile, Southern states brace for heavy rainfall and sudden thunderstorms. These weather shifts could disrupt plans for many Indian travellers this weekend.
Temperatures in Delhi are expected to cross 45 degrees Celsius today. This intense heat poses a high risk for outdoor activities. The IMD suggests staying hydrated and avoiding travel during peak afternoon hours. Local authorities advise people to remain indoors to prevent heat-related health issues.
Delhi Heatwave and Kerala Rain Alert Impact
Down south, Kerala is under a high alert for heavy rainfall today. Several districts face orange and yellow alerts due to an active monsoon path. This rain increases the risk of flooding in many low-lying areas. Travellers should check local updates before visiting popular hill stations or beaches.
Bengaluru is likely to experience sudden thunderstorms by late evening today. These storms may cause minor delays at Kempegowda International Airport (BLR). Similar disruptions are possible at Indira Gandhi International Airport (DEL) due to wind. Railway schedules might also change if tracks face waterlogging or extreme heat.
Bengaluru Thunderstorms and Flight Disruption Watch
Cochin International Airport (COK) and Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (TRV) monitor wind speeds. Low visibility during heavy rain could delay several domestic flights. Road travel in Kerala might be slow today due to potential landslides. Passengers should verify their flight status with airlines before leaving home.
The IMD outlook suggests these conditions will persist for 48 hours. Travellers should carry umbrellas in Kerala and light cotton clothes in Delhi. Staying updated on weather apps will help manage transit delays effectively. Planning ahead ensures a safer journey during this period of extreme weather.
